design_philosophy: Professional, authoritative, and data-dense. The UI must feel like a precision instrument for Kubernetes platform engineers. Muted 'sober' tones (Slates, Zincs) prevent visual fatigue, while high-contrast text ensures instant readability of critical autoscaling data.

typography.font_strategy: The 'Swiss Brutalist' / Technical approach. Using one hyper-legible sans-serif for UI and a distinct monospace for code/metrics.

monospace.usage: For YAML editors, KEDA Scaler metrics, cron expressions, and K8s resource names.

colors.palette_guide: Sober tones. Anchored in Slate/Zinc neutral grays with pure white surfaces and a deep navy/slate-900 primary action color.

spacing_and_layout.grid_strategy: MODE B: THE CONTROL ROOM GRID. Dense, highly scannable, full utilization of screen real estate. Every pixel matters.
spacing_and_layout.page_structure: Fixed left sidebar (navigation) + Top Header (Namespace selector, breadcrumbs, user profile). Main content area scrolls independently.
spacing_and_layout.spacing_scale: Use tight paddings for dense data: p-4 to p-6 for cards. p-8 for main page sections.
spacing_and_layout.radii: Sharp and precise. Use `rounded-md` (6px-8px max) everywhere. No pill-shaped buttons unless it's a status badge.
spacing_and_layout.borders: GRID BORDERS strategy. Expose the skeleton. Use 1px solid `border-slate-200` to separate sections rather than relying heavily on shadows.

login_page.layout: Split screen or centered pristine card. Background should be a subtle CSS pattern (e.g., radial gradient or subtle grid) using Slate 50 to Slate 100. No heavy images.
login_page.form: Shadcn Form, crisp inputs, full-width primary button.

dashboard_overview.layout: Data-heavy table using Shadcn Table. Columns: Name, Namespace, Scaler Type, Min/Max Replicas, Current Status/Active.
dashboard_overview.actions: Inline actions for Edit/Delete on hover.

cron_calendar_view.layout: Custom CSS Grid for the Calendar (7 columns for days). Do NOT use generic date pickers for the macro view.
cron_calendar_view.design: Each day cell must have `border-slate-200`, `min-h-[120px]`. Cron events shown as small colored blocks (e.g., bg-blue-100 text-blue-800) inside the day cell.
cron_calendar_view.interaction: Clicking a day opens a Shadcn Dialog/Sheet to 'Add/Modify Cron Event' (time, timezone, replicas, target ScaledObject).

scaled_object_editor.layout: Two-pane layout. Left: Form/YAML Editor. Right: Live preview/metrics of the ScaledObject.
scaled_object_editor.inputs: Shadcn Select for Scaler Types (supports all KEDA types). Dynamic form fields based on selected Scaler Type.

surfaces.modals: Glassmorphism is BANNED. Use solid white backgrounds with dark overlays (bg-slate-900/40 backdrop-blur-sm).

motion.philosophy: Minimalist and functional. Animations should provide instant feedback without slowing down power users.
motion.hover_states: Buttons translate slightly (`-translate-y-[1px]`) with increased shadow. Rows in tables get `bg-slate-50`.

icons_and_assets.icon_style: Regular or Light weight. Use icons for Scaler Types (e.g., Database for PostgreSQL, Clock for Cron, Message for RabbitMQ).

login_background.description: Use CSS-based subtle grid or radial gradient (`bg-[radial-gradient(ellipse_at_top,_var(--tw-gradient-stops))] from-slate-100 via-slate-50 to-white`) instead of an image to maintain the sober enterprise feel.

instructions_to_main_agent.tech_stack: React (JS/JSX), Tailwind CSS, Shadcn/UI, Recharts (if metrics needed).
instructions_to_main_agent.testing: CRITICAL: You MUST add `data-testid` attributes to all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, calendar days, table rows) formatted as kebab-case role-based names (e.g., `data-testid="login-submit-btn"`).
instructions_to_main_agent.auth: Implement custom JWT auth context wrapping the app.
instructions_to_main_agent.shadcn_customization: Override default Shadcn styles in index.css to match the `--radius: 0.375rem` and exact slate hex codes provided in this guideline.
